name: Stuffed Pumpkin
recipeIngredient: 1 medium pumpkin (12 to 14 inches in diameter)
2 ears corn kernals cut off cob
1 pound firm Chinese tofu, diced and drained
3 tablespoons Braggs Aminos or mild soy sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Wash the pumpkin and cut off the top using a large spoon to scoop out strings and seeds. Mix honey and soy sauce evenly over the insides of the pumpkin. Place 1 cup water in the bottom of a large baking pan, place the top back on the pumpkin, and place it in the baking pan, cover with foil. Bake for 20 minutes or until the pumpkin is just starting to become tender.
2. Meanwhile, prepare the chestnuts by cutting off the shells using a sharp knife. The chestnuts will have a soft brown skin under the shell. Steam the nuts for about 15 minutes, or until they are tender. Rinch them in cool water and slip off the brown skins. Chop the chestnuts coarsely.
3. Combine chestnuts with raisins, apple, celery, onions, corn, green pepper, and brown rice, mixing well.
4. Heat some water in a medium skillet. Add tofu and enough paprika to give it a pleasing color, cooking over high heat for 3 minutes, stirring constantly. Combine the tofu with the rice mixture, mace, Braggs Aminos, and cinnamon, mixing until well blended.
5. Fill the pumpkin with this mixture, replace the top, place it in a baking dish with 1/4 inch water in the bottom. Bake for 45 minutes. Serve on a large platter garnished with parsley, raw cranberries, and orange slices.
